summary.headline: The 2x Rule — With Major Model Exceptions
summary.finding: Most AI models show a price cliff around 2x, but Gemini Flash maintains brand preference even at 3x premium. Model differences grow dramatically at higher prices.
key_insights: Price sensitivity exists (H1 CONFIRMED, p=0.011) but varies significantly by model
At 3x premium, selection rates range from 20.8% (Claude) to 59.4% (Gemini Flash) — 38.6pp spread
Gemini Flash shows NO cliff — maintains brand preference regardless of premium
GPT-5.4 is the strictest 'value calculator' with earliest cliff at 1.75x
Category matters: commodities cliff at 1.2x, electronics tolerate 1.5x
Psychological pricing (.99 endings) has zero effect on AI (H8 CONFIRMED)

gpt54.description: Most price-sensitive. Earliest cliff at 1.75x. Applies strict value analysis.

claude.description: Lowest selection at 3x (20.8%). Shows unique behavior in edge cases.

gemini_pro.description: Clear cliff at 2.0x. Slowest response time (15.8s). Most 'textbook' economic behavior.

gemini_flash.description: NO cliff detected. Maintains 59.4% brand selection even at 3x. May be over-optimized for brands.

model_divergence.conclusion: Models differ SIGNIFICANTLY (p<0.0001). Differences grow with price premium.

position_bias.finding: Being listed first provides a 5.5 percentage point advantage

hypotheses.name: Price sensitivity exists
hypotheses.evidence: 90% of model-product cells show piecewise > linear (9 of 10 non-flat curves)

hypotheses.name: Cliff at 1.25x-2.0x
hypotheses.evidence: Mean breakpoint at 1.94x, but only 33% within strict 1.25-2.0x range (most cluster at ~2.0x)

hypotheses.name: Veblen floor
hypotheses.description: Reduced selection at very low prices
hypotheses.evidence: Only 20% of cells show any floor effect; AI doesn't avoid suspiciously cheap options

hypotheses.name: Model heterogeneity
hypotheses.evidence: Selection rates at 3x range from 20.8% to 59.4% (38.6pp spread, chi-square=197, p<0.0001)

hypotheses.name: No psychological pricing effect
hypotheses.evidence: 100% selection rate across all price formats (standard condition at 1.0x)

hypotheses.name: Justification shifts cliff
hypotheses.evidence: No significant lift from justification at 2.0x (-1.3% difference)

hypotheses.name: Category variation
hypotheses.description: Price sensitivity varies by product category

psych_pricing.result: All formats show identical ~100% selection rate at 1.0x anchor
psych_pricing.finding: AI agents are completely immune to psychological pricing tactics

reasoning_analysis.note: Exploratory analysis from Sub-study D (1,600 trials)

reasoning_condition_divergence.finding: When asked to reason explicitly, GPT-5.4 applies strict value analysis (46.5%) while Gemini Flash maintains brand preference (99.2%)

reasoning_analysis.key_finding: 100% of responses mention price; ~88% mention quality. Value calculation dominates above 2x.

merchant_recommendations.title: Know Your AI Channel
merchant_recommendations.description: Gemini Flash favors brands even at 3x premium (59.4% selection). GPT-5.4 is strictest — stay below 1.75x to maintain advantage. Optimize differently for each AI.

merchant_recommendations.title: Category-Specific Thresholds
merchant_recommendations.description: Commodities (detergent, protein) cliff at 1.2x. Electronics and skincare tolerate up to 1.5x. Never exceed 2x for any category.

merchant_recommendations.title: Position Matters (+5.5pp)
merchant_recommendations.description: Being listed first provides a 5.5 percentage point selection advantage. Optimize structured data and third-party presence for primacy.

merchant_recommendations.title: Skip Charm Pricing
merchant_recommendations.description: Don't waste effort on $X.99 endings. AI agents calculate true value — the extra cent provides zero lift.

merchant_recommendations.title: Quality Claims Won't Save Premium Pricing
merchant_recommendations.description: Justifying higher prices with quality descriptions doesn't move the cliff. Price sensitivity is structural, not persuadable.
